title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 10 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year eurobond on 10 February 2026 to finance its foreign currency reserves, with a 3.5% coupon maturing on 18 February 2029. This was the first 2026 operation under the Bank's updated issuance programme, which shifted to targeting two benchmark issuances annually announced in September 2025.
key_points: Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year RegS/144A eurobond on 10 February 2026 to finance foreign currency reserves
Bond carries a coupon of 3.5% per annum with maturity on 18 February 2029
Joint Lead Managers were RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley
This was the first 2026 operation under the updated issuance programme announced 23 September 2025
BoE shifted to targeting two benchmark issuances annually with regular timetable and high transparency
Foreign exchange reserves are separate from Government-owned reserves managed by BoE as Treasury's agent
why_it_matters: This issuance demonstrates the Bank of England's regularised approach to financing its foreign currency reserves, providing transparency to markets on its reserve management operations. The US$2.5 billion benchmark size and participation of major banks signals continued BoE presence in the eurobond market.
market_structure_context: BoE foreign currency reserves are financed through benchmark eurobond issuances following a regularised two-per-year schedule announced in September 2025. This structural change improved transparency around reserve management operations.
notes: Source is a factual market notice documenting a completed bond issuance operation. The published_at date (2026-03-27) reflects when the notice was made public on the BoE website, while the operation itself occurred on 10 February 2026. Content is thin as a research note due to extensive boilerplate legal disclaimers.
